name: IBM QRadar API
what_it_does: Enterprise SIEM platform with REST API for log management, offense tracking, rule management, and forensic investigation across on-premises and SaaS deployments.
use_cases: Query and triage offenses (correlated incidents) programmatically from SOC automation agents
Search log events and flows via Ariel Query Language (AQL) for threat hunting
Manage SIEM rules and building blocks through the API
Ingest structured threat intelligence to enrich offense context
Automate offense closing and annotation workflows from SOAR platforms
not_for: Organizations without existing QRadar licenses — no self-serve sign-up or free tier
Cloud-native-only shops preferring managed SIEM without on-prem infrastructure
best_when: Best when your organization already runs QRadar and you need to build automation agents that interact with its offense and log management workflows.
avoid_when: Avoid when starting fresh without an existing QRadar deployment, or when modern cloud-native SIEM features (e.g., UEBA, native SOAR) are a priority.

auth.notes: API token (SEC header) or HTTP Basic Auth supported. Authorized Service tokens can be scoped to specific capability sets.

pricing.paid_tiers: License-based pricing per events-per-second (EPS) and flows-per-minute (FPM); SaaS option available via IBM Cloud
pricing.notes: On-premises requires hardware/VM provisioning plus IBM license. SaaS version available but still enterprise-contracted.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests/minute per API token; Ariel search queries are async and results must be polled

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured error objects with HTTP status codes and descriptions; AQL query errors are sometimes cryptic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read endpoints are safe to retry. Offense update and close operations should be treated as non-idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Ariel search queries are asynchronous — agents must poll a search ID for results rather than receiving them inline
API version must be specified in the Accept header or requests default to an older version
Rate limit of 100 req/min can be hit quickly in high-frequency polling loops
On-prem deployments may have self-signed TLS certificates requiring custom CA trust configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized for government deployments. On-prem option means data never leaves the environment. TLS enforcement varies by deployment configuration.
